digest.tldr: We shipped two releases this week, including a minor outage that affected 12 enterprise customers for 47 minutes and is now fully resolved. The team is also making behind-the-scenes improvements to performance and security that directly support the recurring tasks redesign shipping next week.

what_shipped.item: Custom fields v2 (v2.4.0) — customers can now create text, number, and date custom fields on tasks
what_shipped.customer_impact: Enterprise and mid-market customers can now add structured data to tasks — a top feature request from 12% of NPS respondents.

what_shipped.item: Project template library (v2.4.0) — customers can create and reuse project structures
what_shipped.customer_impact: Reduces new project setup time from ~15 minutes to under 2 minutes for teams with standard workflows.

what_shipped.item: Emergency patch v2.4.1 — dashboard restored for enterprise accounts with 200+ projects
what_shipped.customer_impact: The 12 enterprise accounts that could not load their dashboard for 47 minutes are back to full functionality.

what_broke_and_is_fixed.incident: INC-089: Enterprise dashboard outage (47 minutes, July 12, root cause: missing database query limit in v2.4.1)
what_broke_and_is_fixed.customer_impact: 12 enterprise accounts could not access their main dashboard. Fully resolved. Engineering added monitoring to detect this pattern before future deployments.

what_engineering_is_doing.work: Migrating authentication to use non-blocking credential hashing (PR-1847)
what_engineering_is_doing.why_it_matters: Reduces the risk of the login system slowing down all other features during periods of high sign-in traffic. Removes a known reliability risk.

what_engineering_is_doing.work: Improving performance of the task list for large projects (PR-1851)
what_engineering_is_doing.why_it_matters: Directly addresses the UI jank experienced by team leads with 200+ tasks. Unblocks the large-project market segment.

what_engineering_is_doing.work: Building a task dependency graph API (PR-1855)
what_engineering_is_doing.why_it_matters: Backend foundation for the Gantt view and critical path features on the Q4 roadmap.

digest.next_week_preview: v2.5.0 ships this week — the recurring tasks button moves to the task header (the highest-priority user research finding from Q3). 3 engineers dedicated. Target: Wednesday.
digest.action_required_from_pm: Prepare CSM team for v2.5.0 launch — they need updated talking points on recurring tasks discoverability before the release. See #79 Research Insight Broadcaster for the approved talking points.
